scenes.vo: June 27th, 2017. In seventeen minutes, the world's largest shipping company is about to lose every computer it owns. This is the story of NotPetya — the most destructive cyberattack in history.
scenes.title: NOTPETYA
scenes.subtitle: The most destructive cyberattack in history

scenes.vo: The weapon wasn't built by criminals. EternalBlue — an exploit developed inside the NSA — was stolen, and dumped on the internet. Microsoft had shipped a patch. Much of the world never applied it. WannaCry was the warning shot. The world ignored it.
scenes.heading: Built by a state. Lost by a state.

nodes.title: NSA builds EternalBlue
nodes.sub: A Windows exploit, weaponized in secret

nodes.title: Microsoft patches
nodes.sub: MS17-010 ships — much of the world won’t apply it

nodes.title: Dumped online
nodes.sub: The exploit becomes free for anyone

scenes.vo: June 27th. Russian military hackers detonate a poisoned update inside M.E.Doc — the tax software used by nearly every company in Ukraine. One trusted supplier. Within an hour, ten percent of the country's computers are wiped. Banks. Airports. Even Chernobyl's radiation monitors.
scenes.heading: One poisoned update
scenes.statLabel: of all computers in Ukraine — wiped within the hour

scenes.vo: And the worm doesn't stop at the border. It rides every V P N it finds. Maersk loses forty-nine thousand laptops — in seventeen minutes. Merck halts vaccine production. FedEx. Saint-Gobain. Mondelez. None of them were targets. All of them were collateral damage.
scenes.heading: The worm ignores borders

scenes.vo: Maersk survives because of one domain controller in Ghana — offline that day, during a power outage. From that single surviving server, the entire company is rebuilt in ten days — running on WhatsApp, and paper.
scenes.heading: Saved by an accident
scenes.sub: a domain controller in Ghana — offline during a power outage

scenes.vo: There was never a ransom. NotPetya was a wiper, dressed as cybercrime. Total damage: over ten billion dollars. Five nations name the attacker: Russian military intelligence. Insurers call it an act of war — and refuse to pay. Merck fights five years for its one point four billion.
scenes.heading: Not crime. A weapon.

facts.sub: Recovery was never possible. The ransom note was a disguise.
insurers refuse to pay — Merck fights 5 years to win its $1.4B

scenes.vo: Three lessons for the board. You don't have to be the target, to be destroyed. Your most trusted supplier can be the way in. And resilience can hang on a single offline server. NotPetya wasn't the last of its kind. It was the first.
scenes.eyebrow: What this means for the board

lessons.text: You don’t have to be the target to be destroyed.
Your most trusted supplier can be the way in.
Resilience can hang on a single offline server.
